title: arctostaphylos tomentosa subsp. bracteosa
intro: Arctostaphylos tomentosa subsp. bracteosa, also known as the Bigberry Manzanita, is a woody shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant is a member of the heath family and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Arctostaphylos tomentosa subsp. bracteosa include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support immune function, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Arctostaphylos tomentosa subsp. bracteosa is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, and is also used in topical applications, such as salves and creams, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
